content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me examine atom N:14 (nitrogen, blue sphere) more carefully to identify all its covalent neighbors.

From the image, I can see that atom N:14 (nitrogen) is connected to:
- C:13 (carbon, yellow/orange sphere) - directly bonded
- N:15 (nitrogen, blue sphere) - directly bonded  
- H:17 (hydrogen, white sphere) - directly bonded

Looking at the structure more carefully, I can see that N:14 has three bonds:
1. To C:13 (carbon)
2. To N:15 (nitrogen) 
3. To H:17 (hydrogen)

The geomconf_index is 435, as indicated by the file name "geomconf_435.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom 14 (N) are:
- Atom 13 (C)
- Atom 15 (N)
- Atom 17 (H)

Let me confirm this is correct by checking if there are any other connections I might have missed.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 14, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
